annotations.label: Synthetic geometry

anchor.snippet: is geometry without the use of coordinates

annotations.note: Synthetic (coordinate-free axiomatic) geometry as a discipline is a meta-mathematical notion and has no formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Klein's characterization of synthetic geometry

anchor.snippet: Synthetic geometry is that which studies figures as such, without recourse to formulae

annotations.note: Klein's informal characterization of synthetic vs analytic geometry is a quotation, not a formalizable mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Artin's equivalence of synthetic and analytic geometry

anchor.snippet: The fact that the two approaches are equivalent has been proved by Emil Artin

annotations.note: Artin's coordinatization theorem (from Geometric Algebra) recovering an analytic model from synthetic axioms is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Primitives

anchor.snippet: Primitives are the most basic ideas

annotations.note: The methodological notion of a 'primitive' in an axiomatic system is meta-mathematical and not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Axioms

anchor.snippet: Axioms are statements about these primitives

annotations.note: The meta-mathematical concept of 'axiom' is not a Mathlib definition.

annotations.label: Incidence axiom example

anchor.snippet: any two points are together incident with just one line

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Configuration.ProjectivePlane` and related `Configuration.Nondegenerate` capture incidence axioms including unique lines through two points, though not stated as a free-standing axiom.

annotations.label: Theorem

anchor.snippet: When a significant result is proved rigorously, it becomes a theorem

annotations.note: The meta-mathematical notion of 'theorem' is a Lean keyword, not a Mathlib definition.

anchor.snippet: Simply discarding it gives absolute geometry

annotations.label: Hyperbolic geometry (from negation of parallel postulate)

anchor.snippet: while negating it yields hyperbolic geometry

annotations.note: Beyond an incidental mention of the upper half-plane as a model, Mathlib has no axiomatic 'hyperbolic geometry' definition.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic geometry and angle of parallelism

anchor.snippet: where parallel lines have an angle of parallelism that depends on their separation

annotations.note: No occurrence of 'angle of parallelism' or related hyperbolic-geometry theorems found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Elliptic geometry as a case of Riemannian geometry

anchor.snippet: constructed Riemannian geometry , of which elliptic geometry is a particular case

annotations.note: No file in `Mathlib/` mentions 'elliptic geometry'; the relationship to Riemannian geometry is not formalized.

annotations.label: von Staudt: algebraic axioms from incidence

anchor.snippet: algebraic axioms, such as commutativity and associativity of addition and multiplication, were in fact consequences of incidence of lines

annotations.note: Von Staudt's derivation of field operations from projective incidence is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hilbert on Desargues configuration
annotations.note: No 'Desargues' theorem appears in `Mathlib/`; Hilbert's coordinatization result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Affine geometry from parallel lines as primary

anchor.snippet: When parallel lines are taken as primary, synthesis produces affine geometry

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes `AffineSpace` analytically (as a torsor over a vector space) but not synthetic affine geometry built from parallelism axioms.

annotations.label: Synthetic differential geometry

anchor.snippet: Synthetic differential geometry is an application of topos theory to the foundations of differentiable manifold theory

annotations.note: No mention of 'synthetic differential' or SDG-style topos foundations for manifolds in Mathlib.
